name: Nut Wedges
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Finely chopped nuts (walnuts, hazelnuts, pecans)
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: riacmt@ubvmsb.cc.buffalo.edu (Carol Miller-Tutzauer)
1. Stir together the pie crust mix and 1/4 cup sugar.
2. Add 3 to 4 tablespoons water, stirring until a ball forms.
3. Divide into two balls. On a floured surface, roll each ball into a 9-inch circle.
4. Spray an air-insulated cookie sheet with PAM or other non-stick spray.
5. Transfer one circle to the prepared cookie sheet.
6. Preheat oven to 375°F.
7. For filling, combine 1/3 cup sugar, cinnamon, honey, lemon juice, and chopped nuts in a bowl.
8. Spread evenly over the pastry, leaving about 3/4-inch to 1-inch edge without topping.
9. Brush beaten egg white around the edge of the pastry round (acts as glue).
10. Place the second circle on top and gently press down to seal the edges using fork tines.
11. Brush the entire surface with milk for a shiny finish.
12. Bake in the preheated oven at 375°F for 15 to 20 minutes, or until the pastry starts to brown.
13. Allow cooling for 10 minutes before cutting into wedges.
14. Combine chocolate pieces and shortening; melt over low heat until just melted.
15. Drizzle the melted chocolate mixture over the cooled wedges.
